task: Write a Python function median(nums) returning the middle value of the sorted list when the length is odd, or the average of the two middle values as a float when the length is even. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The provided Python function accurately calculates the median of a given list of numbers.
trace: The function first checks if the input list is empty and returns None in that case. Then it sorts the list and calculates the middle index, considering edge cases where the length of the sorted list is even or odd. If the length is even, it averages the two middle values; otherwise, it returns the single middle value as a float. The solution is concise, readable, and handles an edge case by checking if the length of the sorted list is even or odd.
